label: Adaptive chrome color from the active page. Settings marked “restart” take effect after Zen is restarted; the rest apply instantly.
Tint strength (0–100): page color laid as an overlay over your Zen theme. 100 = full page color, 0 = your untouched Zen theme/gradient.
Saturation (0–200): vibrancy of the tint. 0 = grayscale, 100 = the page's own, 200 = double.
Minimum lightness (0–100): floor so near-black pages don't make the chrome muddy. 0 = no floor.
Maximum lightness (0–100): cap so blinding-white pages don't wash the chrome out. 100 = no cap.
Disable on these sites (comma-separated hosts; supports *.example.com). The chrome keeps your Zen theme on them.
Frame gap (px): space between the content area and the window edge.
Live mode — follow video / animated pages (restart)
Enable live mode (continuously follow video / animated content).
Always poll, not only while a video is playing (higher idle cost).
Idle poll interval (ms). Lower = more responsive, more CPU.
Re-tint threshold (0–255): minimum per-channel color change needed to re-tint while polling.
Color fade duration (ms) applied to every tint change.
Always-on host allowlist (comma-separated) for canvas/WebGL players and cross-origin video embeds. Supports *.example.com.
Debug logging to the Browser Console (Cmd/Ctrl-Shift-J).
